name: azure-blob-storage
description: Browse containers and read, upload, and delete blobs in an Azure Storage account

requiredApis.name: Microsoft Entra app registration with Azure Storage user_impersonation permission

envVars.name: MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: Microsoft Azure App Client ID (Application ID)

envVars.name: AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT
envVars.description: Default Azure Storage account name (the {accountName} in https://{accountName}.blob.core.windows.net)

tools.name: List Containers
tools.description: List blob containers in a storage account; the response is XML (EnumerationResults), not JSON

prefix.description: Only return containers whose names begin with this prefix

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

tools.name: List Blobs
tools.description: List blobs in a container, optionally filtered by name prefix; the response is XML (EnumerationResults), not JSON

prefix.description: Only return blobs whose names begin with this prefix, e.g. 'reports/2026/'

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

tools.name: Download Blob
tools.description: Download a blob's content (Get Blob); blob properties are returned as x-ms-* response headers

blobName.description: Blob name, URL-encoded if it contains special characters (slashes for virtual folders may stay as-is)

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

tools.name: Upload Blob
tools.description: Create or overwrite a block blob with the given content (Put Blob)

blobName.description: Blob name to create, including any virtual folder prefix (e.g. reports/2026/summary.csv)

x-ms-blob-type.description: Blob type to create; BlockBlob is correct for ordinary file uploads

contentType.description: MIME type stored as the blob's content type

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

content.description: Raw blob content sent as the request body

tools.name: Delete Blob
tools.description: Delete a blob from a container (Delete Blob); errors are returned as XML

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

tools.name: Create Container
tools.description: Create a new blob container in the storage account (Create Container); fails with 409 if a container with the same name already exists

containerName.description: Container name to create (lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only)

x-ms-blob-public-access.description: Optional public access level: 'container' (full public read) or 'blob' (public blob read); omit to keep the container private

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

tools.name: Copy Blob
tools.description: Server-side copy of a blob to a destination within the storage account (Copy Blob) — no download/re-upload needed; returns 202 with x-ms-copy-status 'success' or 'pending' (poll the destination blob until the copy finishes)

blobName.description: Destination blob name, including any virtual folder prefix

x-ms-copy-source.description: URL of the source blob, e.g. https://{accountName}.blob.core.windows.net/{container}/{blob} (URL-encoded); sources in other accounts must be public or carry a SAS token

x-ms-version.description: Azure Storage service version (YYYY-MM-DD); required for OAuth-authorized requests

prompts.title: Browse a container
prompts.prompt: List the blobs in one of my Azure Storage containers and summarize what's stored there.

prompts.title: Read a stored file
prompts.prompt: Download a blob from my Azure Storage container and summarize its contents.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Azure Blob Storage setup

steps.title: Create a storage account
steps.description: In https://portal.azure.com (a free Azure account includes credit and free-tier storage) create a Storage account, then create a container and upload a small test blob. Note the storage account name — it forms the request host https://{accountName}.blob.core.windows.net.

steps.title: Register a Microsoft Entra application
steps.description: Open https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps/ApplicationsListBlade, register an app (supported account types: accounts in any organizational directory and personal accounts work for the common endpoint), and add your callback URL as a Web redirect URI. Create a client secret. Store the Application (client) ID as MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID and the secret as MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET.

steps.title: Add the Azure Storage delegated permission
steps.description: In the app registration, go to API permissions, click Add a permission, choose Azure Storage, and add the delegated user_impersonation permission.

steps.title: Grant the signed-in user a data role
steps.description: OAuth tokens act on behalf of the user, so the user also needs RBAC access to blob data: on the storage account, assign Storage Blob Data Reader (read-only) or Storage Blob Data Contributor (read/write/delete) to the user under Access control (IAM). Role assignments can take a few minutes to propagate.

steps.title: Set the default account name
steps.description: Optionally set AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT to your storage account name so agents know which account to target; the account name is passed as the accountName parameter on every tool call.

notes: The Blob service REST API returns XML, not JSON — listing responses are <EnumerationResults> documents and errors are <Error> documents.
Every OAuth-authorized request must include an x-ms-version header (2017-11-09 or later); tools default to 2026-04-06, the latest fully deployed service version.

Entra ID authorization is two-layered: the OAuth scope (user_impersonation) only delegates the user's rights, and the user must separately hold a Storage Blob Data RBAC role on the account or container.
The host is account-specific (https://{accountName}.blob.core.windows.net), so every tool takes the storage account name as a parameter.
